---
title: "Reproducible Research: Peer Assessment 1"
output: 
  html_document:
    keep_md: true
---

## Setup and Global Options
First, we will set up the environment and ensure that all code chunks are visible by setting `echo = TRUE`, as required by the assignment.

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)


#1. Loading and preprocessing the data
# Unzip and load the dataset
if(!file.exists("activity.csv")) {
  unzip("activity.zip")
}
activity_data <- read.csv("activity.csv")

# Convert date column from character to Date type
activity_data$date <- as.Date(activity_data$date, format="%Y-%m-%d")

##2. What is mean total number of steps taken per day?

#1. Calculate the total number of steps taken per day and plot a histogram:
# Calculate total steps per day
daily_steps <- activity_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(steps)) %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarize(total_steps = sum(steps))

# Plot the histogram
ggplot(daily_steps, aes(x = total_steps)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "steelblue", color = "black", bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "Histogram of Total Steps per Day", x = "Total Steps", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()

#2. Calculate and report the mean and median of the total number of steps taken per day:
mean_steps <- mean(daily_steps$total_steps)
median_steps <- median(daily_steps$total_steps)

cat("Mean total number of steps per day:", mean_steps, "\n")
cat("Median total number of steps per day:", median_steps, "\n")


###3. What is the average daily activity pattern?

#1. Make a time series plot:
# Calculate average steps per interval
interval_steps <- activity_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(steps)) %>%
  group_by(interval) %>%
  summarize(avg_steps = mean(steps))

# Plot time series
ggplot(interval_steps, aes(x = interval, y = avg_steps)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkred") +
  labs(title = "Average Daily Activity Pattern", x = "5-Minute Interval", y = "Average Number of Steps") +
  theme_minimal()

#2. Which 5-minute interval contains the maximum number of steps?
    max_interval <- interval_steps$interval[which.max(interval_steps$avg_steps)]
max_steps <- max(interval_steps$avg_steps)

cat("The 5-minute interval with the maximum average steps is:", max_interval, "\n")


####4. Imputing missing values

#1. Calculate total number of missing values:
total_nas <- sum(is.na(activity_data$steps))
cat("Total number of missing values in the dataset:", total_nas, "\n")

#2 & 3. Devise a strategy and create a new dataset with filled-in missing values:

# Join the original data with the interval averages, then replace NAs
imputed_data <- activity_data %>%
  left_join(interval_steps, by = "interval") %>%
  mutate(steps = ifelse(is.na(steps), avg_steps, steps)) %>%
  select(-avg_steps) # Drop the helper column

#4. Make a histogram and calculate the new mean and median:
  # Calculate new daily totals
new_daily_steps <- imputed_data %>%
  group_by(date) %>%
  summarize(total_steps = sum(steps))

# Plot new histogram
ggplot(new_daily_steps, aes(x = total_steps)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "forestgreen", color = "black", bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "Histogram of Total Steps per Day (Imputed Data)", x = "Total Steps", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal()

# Calculate new mean and median
new_mean_steps <- mean(new_daily_steps$total_steps)
new_median_steps <- median(new_daily_steps$total_steps)

cat("New Mean:", new_mean_steps, "\n")
cat("New Median:", new_median_steps, "\n")

#####5. Are there differences in activity patterns between weekdays and weekends?

#1. Create a new factor variable indicating weekday/weekend:
# Create the day_type factor variable
imputed_data <- imputed_data %>%
  mutate(day_type = ifelse(weekdays(date) %in% c("Saturday", "Sunday"), "Weekend", "Weekday")) %>%
  mutate(day_type = as.factor(day_type))


#2. Make a panel plot:

# Aggregate by interval and day_type
interval_day_steps <- imputed_data %>%
  group_by(interval, day_type) %>%
  summarize(avg_steps = mean(steps))

# Create the panel plot using ggplot2 facet_grid
ggplot(interval_day_steps, aes(x = interval, y = avg_steps, color = day_type)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_grid(day_type ~ .) +
  labs(title = "Activity Patterns: Weekdays vs. Weekends", 
       x = "5-Minute Interval", 
       y = "Average Number of Steps") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
